data.title: Torrid becomes the latest retailer to suffer consumer snub
data.description:
Torrid (NYSE:CURV) shares are circling the drain near the overnight low as a variety of factors including the election, a more discerning consumer, two hurricanes in October, and a lack of new offerings were blamed for a very disappointing quarter and an underwhelming outlook for the current quarter and remainder of the year.

The results echo similar sentiments from other retailers that have seen sales and profitability hurt by the compromised economic environment and consumers become more discriminating on how they spend fewer discretionary dollars.

The company’s CEO acknowledge the company’s shortcomings last quarter, namely the lack of “newness and novelty” in the fall core collection.

“We saw softness in our core collection, which was not innovative enough in an environment where consumer spending was constrained,” CEO Lisa Harper said on the company’s earnings call, adding that Torrid now recognizes that the core product was too similar to what had been offered, and did not infuse it with enough freshness and fashion. Said differently, “It was too much of the same thing she already had in her closet.”

“The new assortment could provide life to the brand and potentially lift comparable sales and leverage in 2025,” Seeking Alpha analyst Quipus Capital writes. But even with a 30% reversal, the stock is still not priced attractively, at least not until Q1 20225 when the new assortments potentially revive the brand.

“Although Torrid stock is cheaper than it has ever been since [April 2024] I now only believe the name is barely fairly priced, but not an opportunity. Below $3, and particularly below $2.50, I would become much more interested in Torrid,” Quipus Capital adds.

This would imply another 17% decline in the stock, on top of the 27% drop since Tuesday’s close.

Related tickers: Kohl's (KSS), Target (TGT), Guess? (GES), Shoe Carnival (SCVL)

data.title: Five Below Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.25, revenue of $843.7M beats by $42.72M
data.description:
• Five Below press release (NASDAQ:FIVE): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.25.
  • Revenue of $843.7M (+14.6% Y/Y) beats by $42.72M.

  • For the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2024:

  • Net sales are expected to be in the range of $1.35 billion to $1.38 billion based on opening approximately 22 net new stores and assumes an approximate 3% to 5% decrease in comparable sales.

  • Net income is expected to be in the range of $174 million to $184 million. Adjusted net income(3) is expected to be in the range of $179 million to $189 million.

  • Diluted income per common share is expected to be in the range of $3.15 to $3.33 on approximately 55.3 million diluted weighted average shares outstanding. Adjusted diluted income per common share is expected to be in the range of $3.23 to $3.41.

  • For the full year of Fiscal 2024:

  • Net sales are expected to be in the range of $3.84 billion to $3.87 billion based on opening approximately 227 net new stores and assumes an approximate 3% decrease in comparable sales.
  • Net income is expected to be in the range of $240 million to $250 million. Adjusted net income is expected to be in the range of $265 million to $275 million.
  • Diluted income per common share is expected to be in the range of $4.34 to $4.52 on approximately 55.3 million diluted weighted average shares outstanding. Adjusted diluted income per common share is expected to be in the range of $4.78 to $4.96.
  • Gross capital expenditures are expected to be approximately $340 million in fiscal 2024.

data.title: 'We don't have any fears," Rio Tinto CEO says of incoming Trump administration
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) expects the upcoming change in U.S. political leadership to offer potential upside as well as downside, CEO Jakob Stausholm said Wednesday at the company's investor seminar in London.

"We don't have any fears," the CEO said, even after President-elect Trump recently rattled markets with talk of higher tariffs on all goods from Canada and China, noting the previous Trump administration was fruitful to the company.

Rio Tinto (RIO) produces most of its aluminum in Canada, while 14.5% of the company is owned by a Chinese state-owned aluminum firm.

Rio (RIO) shares wobble between small gains and losses in Wednesday's trading after forecasting little change in iron ore shipments from its Australian operations for 2025, while expecting Y/Y gains in copper and bauxite production.

While Rio's (RIO) profits flow mostly from iron ore, the miner is increasing its focus on copper, whose demand is expected to benefit from the energy transition.

Rio (RIO) is targeting copper production of 1M tons/year by the end of the decade, underpinned by rising output from the Oyu Tolgoi mine in Mongolia, where production is expected to jump by more than 50% next year.

Citi analysts say Rio's (RIO) guidance update looks largely in line with expectations, with iron ore shipment targets seen coming in flat next year - slightly lower than Citi's forecast - while Rio sees $11B in capital spending for 2025, up from $9.5B in 2024 and $800M more than Citi had expected.

data.title: GoDaddy gets up after showing off Airo monetization strategy
data.description:
GoDaddy (NYSE:GDDY) shares moved higher Wednesday after the web hosting company showcased the monetization strategy behind Airo Plus, its artificial intelligence-powered web building platform.

Shares climbed 4% by late afternoon trading. GoDaddy held an investor dinner on Tuesday that detailed a new $5 per month subscription plan for Airo Plus. The subscription provides access to various functionalities, including a premium logo maker, insights around the user's site and social media.

GoDaddy claims Airo Plus can design a website, logo and create an email and social campaign in the time it takes to read a single sentence.

"We took initial pricing as a reflection of feedback the company has seen from customer testing, but we believe that between the value being provided, combined with the additional tools which can drive further attach of other products, the value creation from Airo Plus could be substantial over time, and we'd think the monthly price could move up accordingly," said RBC analyst Brad Erickson, in an investor note on Wednesday.

Following the presentation, RBC increased its price target on the stock to $230 from $190 and maintained its Outperform rating.

"Airo Plus as a premium offering shows a clearer path to monetization, and while we think it’ll take some time to show up in numbers, the low initial price​-​point for a number of features will likely see decent uptake," Barclays analysts said.

data.title: Shift4 Payments downgraded to Neutral as CEO nominated to head NASA
data.description:
Bank of America Securities downgraded Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) to Neutral from Buy as President-elect Donald Trump's announcement that he'll nominate CEO Jared Isaacman for NASA administrator leads to uncertainty over FOUR's leadership.

Shift4 (NYSE:FOUR) stock dropped 12% in late Wednesday trading.

Isaacman's unexpected departure "could become an unwelcomed distraction that may negatively impact execution," BofA analyst Jason Kupferberg wrote in a note to clients.

Still, he noted that Isaacman intends to remain CEO until his confirmation and stay fully engaged at the company's investor day in February.

In a letter to Shift4 (FOUR) employees, the CEO said he plans to keep his majority interest in the company (subject to ethics obligations), and will reduce his voting power to be commensurate with other class A shareholders, Kupferberg said. As of Dec. 31, 2023, Isaacman controlled ~80% of voting power in the company across multiple share classes. He holds less than 1% of publicly traded A shares.

Kupferberg expects that company President Taylor Lauber wll be the logical choice to succeed Isaacman.

The analyst lowered his price target on Shift4 (FOUR) to $105 from $112.

BofA's Neutral rating aligns with the SA Quant rating of Hold and diverges from the average Wall Street rating of Strong Buy and the average SA Analyst rating of Buy.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Wisconsin energy companies delay suspension of coal-fired operations to 2029
data.description:
Columbia Energy Center co-owners Alliant Energy (NASDAQ:LNT), Madison Gas and Electric Company (MGEE) and Wisconsin Public Service Corp. (NYSE:WEC) said Wednesday they plan to delay the suspension of coal operations to year-end 2029 from the previous date of 2026.

The companies said the delay provides time to explore converting at least one of Columbia's units to natural gas before the end of 2029, while also furthering reliability and maintaining affordability.

The move is the latest example of the slowdown in planned closures of coal-fired power plants in the U.S. as forecasts show rising power demand from data centers and manufacturing.

Utilities will retire just 3.0 GW of U.S. coal-fired capacity in 2024, the lowest level since 2015, according to an analysis by S&P Global.

ETF: (XLU)

data.title: OpenAI partners with defence company Anduril to build AI for military use
data.description:
ChatGPT-maker OpenAI and defence technology company Anduril said on Wednesday that they are partnering to develop and deploy advanced artificial intelligence (AI) solutions for national security missions, the first significant move for the AI giant’s foray into the defence sector.

The partnership will focus on improving the nation’s counter-unmanned aircraft systems (CUAS) and their ability to detect, assess and respond to potentially lethal aerial threats in real-time.

As part of the initiative, Anduril and OpenAI will explore how AI models can be leveraged to synthesize time-sensitive data, reduce the burden on human operators, and improve situational awareness, the companies said.

These AI models will be trained on Anduril’s library of data on CUAS threats and operations.

"Our partnership with Anduril will help ensure OpenAI technology protects U.S. military personnel, and will help the national security community understand and responsibly use this technology to keep our citizens safe and free," OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said.

Anduril, a leading defence technology startup, was last valued at $14B, and makes reusable rockets, drones and submarines.

OpenAI’s foray into the defence sector comes after its rival, Anthropic announced a similar partnership last month with Palantir (PLTR) to provide U.S. intelligence and defence agencies access to its Claude 3 and 3.5 family of AI models.

Palantir (PLTR) shares were down more than 3.2% in afternoon trading on Wednesday.

data.title: French government topples as parliament votes to oust Prime Minister (Update)
data.description:
The French government toppled on Wednesday after the Parliament voted to pass a no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Michel Barnier, plunging the eurozone’s second-biggest economy into a deeper political crisis.

French lawmakers voted 331 out of 574 current members supporting the no-confidence motion.

New parliamentary elections cannot take place until next June-July, 12 months on from the last vote.

The challenge to Barnier’s government stemmed from opposition to his proposed budget to reduce France’s massive fiscal deficit. The no-confidence motion marks the first time in over 60 years that a French government has been ousted through such a motion.

This follows months of political uncertainty in France, starting when President Emmanuel Macron called for a snap election in June, which resulted in a hung parliament.

The political turmoil has varying implications for both French and European markets, triggering volatility in both. France's benchmark index CAC 40 (CAC:IND) has shown significant weakness lately, declining over 9% since Macron called for a snap election.

The euro (EUR:USD) has also declined sharply against the U.S. dollar, last down 0.16%, after inching up a little in choppy trading due to a fall in the dollar as chances of a December rate cut firmed. The euro fell 1% to $1.0469 on Tuesday.

French government bonds have also seen higher yields lately as investors demand higher returns for potential risks. The yield spread between French and German 10-year government bonds reached 90 bps last week, its highest level since 2012.

France’s political crisis has reached beyond its borders, harbouring negative sentiment across European markets, with bearish trading signals for European indices. The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) has also ticked lower, weighed down by volatility in France’s CAC 40 Index.

With the no-confidence motion passed against Prime Minister Barnier, markets can expect increased volatility, the immediate concern being the passage of the French budget.

The move could also rattle currency markets, with the euro already faltering against the U.S. Dollar. The European Central Bank (ECB)’s monetary policies will also be influenced by the uncertain market sentiment.

French politics will remain in limbo as there can be no new parliamentary election before July and a new Prime Minister would face the same challenges as Barnier to get the budget adopted by a divided parliament.

This story has been updated to add that the French parliament has voted in favour of the no-confidence motion against the Prime Minister.

data.title: General Dynamics declares $1.42 dividend, authorizes additional stock buybacks
data.description:
• General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) declared $1.42/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.04%

  • See GD Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Worker strike slows production at B2Gold's flagship Mali mine - Reuters
data.description:
A workers strike at B2Gold's (NYSE:BTG) Fekola mine in Mali has reduced mining operations to just 25% of capacity, Reuters reported Wednesday.

As many as 2,000 workers at the company's flagship mine launched the seven-day strike on November 29 over the dismissal of some of their colleagues, according to the report, which cited two employees.

B2Gold (BTG) said operations at Fekola were continuing at a reduced capacity, although "well in excess" of the 25% reported by the sources.

The company said earlier this week that the strike action was triggered by steps it took against a few employees who had engaged in alleged illegal activities.

The Fekola plant was operating normally and production was expected to come in near the lower end of a previous forecast range of 420K-450K oz of gold this year, the company said.
